## Output Format
Return a single JSON object with these fields:
{
  "dimensions": [list of canonical dimension names],
  "metrics": [list of canonical metric names — absolute/average/cumulative only],
  "formula_metrics": [list of canonical formula/percentage metric names],
  "having": null | {
    "type": "vs_average" | "gap_to_target" | "metric_comparison",
    "polarity": "positive" | "negative" | null,
    "conditions": [{"metric1": "<name>", "operator": "<|>|<=|>=", "metric2": "<name>"}]
  },
  "filters": {"<dimension_name>": "<value>"},
  "date_range": null | {"start": "YYYYMMDD", "end": "YYYYMMDD"},
  "sort": null | {"metric": "<name>", "direction": "ASC|DESC"},
  "limit": null | <integer>,
  "confidence": <float 0.0-1.0>
}

## Rules
- Only use canonical names from the lists above.
- Never include stored percentage columns in metrics — use formula_metrics instead.
- Set confidence < 0.7 if you encounter dimensions or metrics not in the lists.
- having conditions use actual db_column names from the metric definitions.
